zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Alderwick於1379年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷時，她的父親William Alderwick28歲，母親Joan de Saxulby24歲。她於1392年與William Brokesby在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1454年1月在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷享年75歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Alderwick于1379年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷时，她的父亲William Alderwick28岁，母亲Joan de Saxulby24岁。她于1392年与William Brokesby在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1454年1月在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: Alderwick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: apparently from an unidentified place named with Old English alor ‘alder’ + Old English wīc ‘specialized or outlying farm’. However, no medieval bearers have been found to support this, and it is more likely that Alderwick is an altered form of another Wilts name, probably Aldericke. Compare John Aldericke, 1670, John Alderwick, 1679, John Aldric, 1712 in IGI (Broughton Gifford, Wilts), where the spelling -wick appears to be a hypercorrection, mistakenly assuming -ick to be a vulgar pronunciation of -wick. Ald(e)ric(k) ( see Aldrick ) is a variant of Aldridge .

variants.name: IGI
meanings: English: variant of   Wellings  . Compare Edward Wellings, 1662, Humphrey Wellens, 1684 in   IGI   (Wentnor, Shrops).

variants.name: Aldridge
meanings: from the Middle English personal names Alrich or Elrich and Aldrich or Eldrich, representing any one of three Old English personal names, Æthelrīc, Ælfrīc, and Ealdrīc, formed respectively from æthel- ‘noble’, ælf ‘elf, sprite’, and (e)ald ‘old, honored’ + rīc ‘ruler’.

habitational name from Aldridge (Staffordshire), Aldridge Grove in Hampden (Buckinghamshire), or from a similarly named unidentified place in the West Midlands, recorded in Domesday Book as Alrewic, from Old English alor ‘alder’ + wīc ‘specialized farmstead’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Alderwick was born in 1379, in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England, her father, William Alderwick, was 28 and her mother, Joan de Saxulby, was 24. She married William Brokesby in 1392, in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died in January 1454, in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Alderwick est née en 1379, à Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, William Alderwick, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Joan de Saxulby, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé William Brokesby en 1392, à Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en janvier 1454, à Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 75 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Alderwickが1379年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアメルトン モウブレイで生まれた時，父William Alderwickは28歳，母Joan de Saxulbyは24歳であった。1392年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアメルトン モウブレイでWilliam Brokesbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1454年1月にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアメルトン モウブレイで75歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Alderwick(이)가 1379년 영국 레이스스터실르 멜턴 모우브라이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Alderwick(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Saxulby(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1392년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 멜턴 모우브라이에서 William Brokesby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1454년 1월 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 멜턴 모우브라이에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Alderwick nasceu em 1379, em Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Alderwick, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Saxulby, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com William Brokesby em 1392, em Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em janeiro de 1454, em Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 75 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Alderwick родилась в 1379 г. в местности мелтон мавбрэи, лисестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Alderwick было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Saxulby, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Brokesby в 1392 г. в местности мелтон мавбрэи, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла январь 1454 г. в местности мелтон мавбрэи, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Alderwick nació en 1379, en Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Alderwick, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Joan de Saxulby, tenía 24 años. Se casó con William Brokesby en 1392, en Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en enero de 1454, en Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 75 años.
